mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving corporate earnings expectations higher and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 20-day SPX returns during early-cycle expansions, or the signal reverses direction when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with near-zero policy rates creates stagflation expectations, prompting equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 70% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 220 and FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real gross domestic product confirms broad-based economic expansion, boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS and GDPC1 growth rates yields a negative or zero predictive coefficient for 60-day SPX returns in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high unemployment above 8.5% indicates structural labor market weakness that suppresses consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 60% of periods where UNRATE remains above 8.5%, invalidating the bearish labor signal.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production alongside stable consumer prices signals healthy supply-side growth without inflationary pressure, supporting equity risk premiums and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the combined INDPRO and CPIAUCSL signal falls below 0.01 over consecutive 1-year out-of-sample windows, or 5-day forward returns show no directional bias.
